description: Markdown regeneration — surgically revises the newsletter draft based on explicit user feedback while preserving voice, structure, and URL integrity.

prompts.instruction:
<Task_Context>
Surgically revise the existing newsletter Markdown based on explicit user feedback. This is a targeted edit, not a full regeneration.
</Task_Context>

<Original_Markdown>
{original_markdown}
</Original_Markdown>

<User_Feedback>
{user_feedback}
</User_Feedback>

<Revision_Rules>
1. SCOPE: Modify ONLY the sections explicitly mentioned in the feedback.
2. VERBATIM LOCK: Copy all non-referenced sections, headings, URLs, and formatting VERBATIM from the original.
3. VOICE PRESERVATION: Maintain Kevin's calibrated editorial voice (Pattern A, contractions, declarative authority) in revised sections.
4. URL INTEGRITY: Never alter, remove, or fabricate URLs. All links must come from the original data.
5. STRUCTURAL INTEGRITY: Preserve all 8 section headings and their ordering. Do not add or remove sections.
6. CHARACTER LIMITS: If the revision affects a constrained section (e.g., Featured Article paragraphs at 300-400 chars), ensure the revised text still falls within limits.
7. PRIORITY: If the feedback contradicts the original editorial guidelines, follow the feedback.
</Revision_Rules>

<Output_Constraint>
Output ONLY the complete revised newsletter in valid Markdown. No commentary, no explanations, no diff markers.
</Output_Constraint>
